own_review_points: Read-time validateCompression() in getIcebergTableProperties (IcebergUtil:545) throws NOT_SUPPORTED on DESCRIBE/SHOW CREATE for externally-created tables with codecs Trino rejects (e.g. LZ4 on Avro) — read-path regression
Stored codec representation changed from lowercase (old toParquet/AvroCompressionCodecTableProperty + ORC toLowerCase) to uppercase HiveCompressionCodec.name() — possible Spark/Iceberg interop break
Catalog default iceberg.compression-codec no longer persisted to table props when unset; effective compression invisible in SHOW CREATE TABLE
Parquet-only empty-property workaround for issue #20401; ORC/AVRO not given the same treatment
validateCompression duplicated across read, createTableProperties, calculateTableCompressionProperties and IcebergAvroFileWriter ctor (redundant)
setTableProperties: newFileFormat defaults to oldFileFormat (human-ref line 2502) and calculateTableCompressionProperties runs unconditionally, carrying old codec onto new format property
Test column_size assertions 45->51 / 49->52 reflect changed Parquet default-compression behavior
docs: compression_codec added to CREATE-TABLE property table but ALTER-TABLE updatable-properties list (outside this docs hunk) not updated
human-ref nits: static-import AVRO; commit-message wording
notes: Full diff (71k, not truncated). Excellent, well-grounded review. Point 2 (read-time validation breaking external tables) correctly identifies a real read-path regression -> critical. Point 3 (lowercase->uppercase stored codec) verified against removed toParquet/AvroCompressionCodecTableProperty helpers. Point 8 covers the human reference's IcebergMetadata:2502 newFileFormat=oldFileFormat query. No factual errors, no fabrications. Missed only the two human-ref nits (static import AVRO, commit message).
